title: Pyrus × arsenii
taxonomy: Pyrus × arsenii is classified as a hybrid of the Pyrus communis species. The plant is believed to be a cross between Pyrus communis and another species within the Pyrus genus. Due to limited information on this specific hybrid, its classification may be subject to further research. However, its parent species, Pyrus communis, is classified as a member of the Rosaceae family. The exact classification of Pyrus × arsenii may be more complex and require further investigation.

intro: Pyrus × bokjoo (also known as Pyrus × arsenii) is a hybrid of pear tree species, commonly cultivated for its medicinal and edible properties. The plant is valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus × bokjoo include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pyrus × bokjoo is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction, or dried and used as a tea, although its traditional uses also include being used in combination with other herbs in folk remedies and dietary supplements.
